competitors.name: yourhonestybox.com
competitors.target_customer: Irish and NI consumers seeking honesty boxes
competitors.offering: Community-contribution map of honesty boxes in Ireland and Northern Ireland; no commercial tier; no owner dashboard; no farm shops
competitors.pricing_model: Free — zero revenue model; passion project
competitors.strengths: First-mover in Ireland/NI honesty box space; community trust; 336 listings (now Farmmap seed data via consent); no commercial pressure creating user antipathy
competitors.weaknesses: No commercial model; Ireland/NI only; no farm shop vertical; no claiming workflow; no photos or product catalogue; cannot monetise attention
competitors.differentiation_opportunity: Farmmap has commercial model, UK scope, farm shops, owner dashboard, and marketplace capability. Partnership formalised via consent; data creditor not competitor.
competitors.relationship_status: Partner — 336 listings obtained with explicit consent. Formal data licence agreement recommended before V1 launch.

competitors.name: Farm Retail Association Directory (farmerretail.co.uk)
competitors.target_customer: FRA member farm shops (1,200 members); consumers seeking verified farm shops
competitors.offering: Text/list directory gated to FRA members; no map-first; no honesty boxes; no marketplace; directory is a member benefit not a standalone product
competitors.pricing_model: Bundled with FRA membership (£200–£800/year for membership)
competitors.traffic_estimate: 15,000–40,000 monthly sessions (inference; FRA known brand in sector)
competitors.strengths: Institutional credibility; 1,200 established member relationships; 20+ years domain authority; can endorse or block Farmmap credibility with single communication
competitors.weaknesses: Membership-gated — 2,300+ non-member shops invisible; not map-first; no mobile-first; no honesty boxes; no Ireland coverage; no marketplace; trade body pace not product pace
competitors.differentiation_opportunity: Farmmap covers the 2,300+ non-member shops FRA ignores; map-first UX; honesty boxes; Ireland/NI; marketplace. FRA partnership converts biggest threat into highest-value acquisition channel.
competitors.relationship_status: Target for data-sharing partnership. FRA endorsement is highest-priority pre-launch relationship. Posture: complementary service, not competitor.

competitors.name: BigBarn (bigbarn.co.uk)
competitors.category: Local food directory — closest product competitor
competitors.target_customer: UK consumers seeking local food; farm shops, box schemes, farmers markets
competitors.offering: Map component (supplementary, not primary) + producer pages + some listing capability; desktop-first; inadequate mobile experience; no Stripe Connect marketplace
competitors.pricing_model: Premium listing fees estimated £10–£30/month (no public pricing page); free basic listing
competitors.traffic_estimate: 30,000–80,000 monthly sessions (inference; 24-year domain age; strong for 'farm shop near me' queries in many regions)
competitors.strengths: 24 years domain authority; ranks for farm shop discovery queries; known brand in local food community; some FRA relationship
competitors.weaknesses: Desktop-first UX failing modern Core Web Vitals; map supplementary not primary; no honesty boxes; no Ireland/NI; no marketplace; 24 years technical debt; £45k net assets (Companies House) — not resourced for major rebuild
competitors.differentiation_opportunity: Mobile-first modern UX; map-first design; honesty boxes as first-class listing type; Ireland/NI coverage; Stripe Connect marketplace; PostGIS-powered spatial queries; performance dashboard
competitors.relationship_status: Active competitor. Farmmap SEO programme should systematically target BigBarn's current keyword positions. BigBarn domain acquisition opportunistic if commercial trajectory continues declining.

competitors.name: LocalFoodFinder
competitors.offering: Ceased active development; residual search rankings only
competitors.strengths: Historical brand recognition — farm shop owners may cite it alongside other failed directories
competitors.differentiation_opportunity: LocalFoodFinder's exit confirms both the viability challenge (maintaining a local food directory is hard) and the opportunity gap. Farmmap rebuttal: modern map-first product, traffic-first model, data before money.
competitors.relationship_status: Inactive — historical reference point for sales conversations

competitors.name: Farmdrop
competitors.target_customer: Urban consumers seeking farm-direct grocery delivery
competitors.offering: Curated marketplace with own logistics, warehousing, last-mile delivery. Raised £12m VC. Closed February 2022.
competitors.pricing_model: Retail margin model — Farmdrop was the retailer, not a platform
competitors.strengths: Historical case study: Farmdrop's model is not Farmmap's model. The comparison is Farmmap's strongest rebuttal to 'Farmdrop failed, why will you succeed?'
competitors.weaknesses: Failed on last-mile logistics unit economics; unrelated to Farmmap's platform model
competitors.differentiation_opportunity: Farmmap never touches logistics. Stripe Connect Standard makes the farm shop the merchant of record. Platform model vs retail model — structurally different thesis.
competitors.relationship_status: Closed — no threat; strong differentiation narrative

competitors.name: Google Maps
competitors.target_customer: 1 billion+ monthly active users globally (Google I/O 2021)
competitors.offering: Farm shops appear as Google Business Profile listings; no category depth; no honesty boxes; no product catalogue; no marketplace; no farm shop-specific filtering
competitors.pricing_model: Free to business (Google Business Profile); advertising-funded consumer product
competitors.traffic_estimate: 1 billion monthly active users globally (Google I/O 2021)
competitors.strengths: Default consumer discovery platform; consumer trust; search integration; farm shop appears in Google search results via GBP; Google Maps reviews appear in search
competitors.weaknesses: Generic platform; inconsistent farm shop data (43% of farm shops have incorrect/stale Google Maps data — AHDB Farm Business Survey 2023); no honesty box category; no product catalogue; no marketplace; not explorable for new-discovery browsing
competitors.differentiation_opportunity: Category depth: farm shop vs honesty box vs farm gate stall distinction; product catalogue; seasonal availability; marketplace capability; browsable explore experience vs search-only. SEO strategy: rank for queries Google Maps itself answers poorly.
competitors.relationship_status: Distribution partner — primary source of consumer traffic to Farmmap via organic search. Not a product competitor.

competitors.name: Facebook Business Pages
competitors.target_customer: Existing followers of individual farm shop Facebook pages
competitors.offering: Free social presence; updates to existing followers; photos, hours, events. Estimated 60–70% of UK farm shops with digital presence use Facebook (inference — FRA Digital Adoption Survey 2022)
competitors.traffic_estimate: Per-page — highly variable; aggregate Meta platform dominant
competitors.strengths: Dominant farm shop owner attention share; 'I'm already on Facebook and it's free' is hardest sales objection; real engagement with existing customers
competitors.weaknesses: Serves existing audience only; no location-based discovery for new consumers; no honesty box category; no map browsing; no product catalogue; no marketplace; consumer searching 'farm shop near me' does not use Facebook
competitors.differentiation_opportunity: Farmmap finds consumers who have never heard of the farm shop. Facebook serves people who already follow them. Different jobs; not competing tools.
competitors.relationship_status: Incumbent in farm shop owner attention/time. Farmmap sales pitch: 'Leave Facebook for your regulars; use Farmmap for new customers.'

competitors.name: Yelp / TripAdvisor
competitors.target_customer: Consumers seeking reviews of restaurants, attractions, and food experiences
competitors.offering: Consumer reviews; star ratings; some farm shop coverage in tourist-adjacent areas; TripAdvisor reviews appear in Google search results
competitors.traffic_estimate: Global dominant (not farm-shop specific traffic estimable)
competitors.strengths: TripAdvisor reviews carry high trust in Google search results; existing consumer reviews of larger farm shops provide social proof; brand recognition as trusted review platforms
competitors.weaknesses: Incomplete coverage — no honesty boxes; minimal rural small farm coverage; not map-browsable for local food; no farm shop-specific features; no marketplace; neither platform actively curates farm shop data
competitors.differentiation_opportunity: Farmmap owns the farm shop-specific trust layer once F5 (reviews) launches. Until then, a 'See on TripAdvisor' link on claimed listing pages is a bridge solution.
competitors.relationship_status: Partial trust dependency at V1/V2. Farmmap reviews (F5) launched at V2 to break dependency over 36–60 months.

summary_implications: The competitive landscape is structurally fragmented with no single competitor offering the full Farmmap proposition: map-first, farm shops + honesty boxes, UK + Ireland, free browse + subscription + marketplace. The nearest product competitor (BigBarn) has declining commercial health and 24 years of technical debt. The most dangerous institutional threat (FRA) is better managed as a partner. The data rights question with yourhonestybox.com is resolved via consent. Farmmap's 12–18 month execution window before a well-funded entrant can replicate V1 is the primary urgency driver.
